release_notes: Two refinements to the Ready Messages log emphasis. (1) Outgoing Ready lines now insert ABOVE the bolded tier instead of at the bottom of the log. Previously a 'Ready sent' from this workstation pushed the recent1 (newest incoming) and recent2 (second-most-recent incoming) lines off the bottom of the visible log, so the bolded items ended up sitting in the middle and the un-bolded outgoing line was the most-recent visible row — confusing. v1.0.39 pins the two bolded incoming lines to the bottom of the log; outgoing entries slot into the un-bolded older section just above recent2 (or above recent1 if recent2 doesn't exist yet, or at the end if neither does). Timestamps are temporarily out of chronological order until the next incoming Ready arrives, which the user prefers over losing the visual hierarchy. (2) On startup, the restored log now rebuilds the recent1/recent2 emphasis tiers exactly as they were at shutdown. The persistent file format gains a per-line IN/OUT prefix so _load_ready_log can replay each entry through the same code path that handled it live: incoming lines run through the demote-and-tag pipeline (with the flash animation suppressed since cold lines aren't fresh arrivals), and outgoing lines run through the insert-above-emphasis path. Legacy v1.0.38 files without prefixes restore as plain text — a one-time cosmetic gap on upgrade. Internally: _append_ready_log gains a persist=False flag, _append_incoming_ready_log gains animate=False and persist=False flags, _persist_ready_log_line takes a direction='IN'|'OUT' arg, and the recent1_start / recent2_start marks now use right-gravity so an outgoing insert at their position correctly pushes them along with the emphasized content. Only ever two lines are bolded at any time — the demote chain already enforces that.
